feedback.text: As one does not cut it more feedback: - unfair personalisation practices Most Companys try to hide their Support pages, they like you to an FAQ or similar problems site, even if you try to contact their Support directly. They go even so far to redirect you after you described your problem. If a bot detects Words that could fit the FAQ they redirect you. Even if your problem has nothing to do with it. Mainly to cut cost in support staff, If its their mistake they should be there to fix it, not a bot. This shouldn´t even be allowed. - unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns Forced account requirements, most of the time companys try to get your data this way. Buy a product, you can only use all functions if you have an account, even doe the account is not necessery. Most used argument against this by companys "security purposes". This is a straight out lie, and easy to prove. If I dont share my data its save, If im forced to share my data at any point. Its not save anymore, it does not help to foolprove a system, there will always be Human error. As for this its never, save to share my data if I dont have to. Lets take Youtube as an example, in the early days youtube generated your feed without account, through the used of cookies you oppted in or out, they disabled that to force you to login. On the surface you can still use the site of course but they stripped away function after function, while still pretending to be free. If I have to make an account its not free, they sell my data, therefore I have to pay with data. Simple solution for all of this, dont allow it. But then they cant make business, they adapt and they will adapt fast, for 2 reasons. They need the User and they want to make business, they complain yes but they wouldn´t loose alot, because it was functioning before just fine. It functioned good enough to get them this big. What do I mean by this, do not allow a forced account policy. If they want to used it they are barred from selling it, the premise security as mentioned is a lie. If the product can function reasonably without account or there is a system inplace where you bought the product they should not be allowed to enforce a new account. Reasonably means, Standalone Software I buy should not require an account, if the keyvalidation can be concluded otherwise. Possibly ways: Physical Key, Digital Key, Autentificator, E-Mailverification (delete after verification no account needed buying account = validation account), Unique key on purchase as valitation of liecense, phone, sms, whatsapp verification (all three worst methods). Never allow forced online verification, main problem as I would give away my data, security risk. Not every system we use nowadays is there to our benefit, to be honest most of them are there because there because they missused the publics missing knowlegde about technology or mainly the political knowledge, they get played at every turn. Problem is if people like me explain this stuff, most of them cant even comprehend what we are talking about at the same time they feel attacked, instead of registrating that companys use them as mouthpieces. Just sad to wittness, 100 years from now there will be comprehensive studys about this guilible time period.
